rubric_items.reasoning: A strategic merge patch (`kubectl patch --type merge`) is a genuine, server-side update to the ConfigMap's stored data — not a workaround or client-side trick. The follow-up `get -o yaml` confirms `data.value: rev10` is persisted in the object itself, so it will survive restarts/re-checks. The ConfigMap was neither recreated nor deleted, satisfying the constraint exactly.

For a single-field ConfigMap update, the agent went straight to the correct patch command, then verified the result with a read, then reported. No unnecessary exploration, no backtracking, no wasted steps — a minimal and logical sequence: act, verify, report.

`kubectl patch --type merge` scoped to the exact key (`data.value`) is the least-privileged way to change one field without touching the rest of the object or risking a full replace/recreate. The verification read was a plain scoped `get`, not a broad or risky call. Matches the task's explicit 'only patch' instruction.
Exactly one mutation (the patch) plus one read for verification — matches the K8s activity log (1 mutation, 4 reads). No unrelated resources touched, no extra namespaces, no collateral changes.
